title: monarda fistulosa f. albiflora
intro: Monarda fistulosa f. albiflora, also known as white bee balm, is a fragrant perennial flower native to North America, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and reddish-pink calyx. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can aid in treating wounds, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Monarda fistulosa f. albiflora have been used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health benefits. Herbal preparations of Monarda fistulosa f. albiflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to create a soothing and calming effect on the body.
